mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing equity discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5% for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL signal a tightening cycle with stagflation risks, causing S&P 500 forward returns to decline over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL exceed thresholds for 2 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals weak supply chain capacity, reducing corporate earnings growth and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative despite INDPRO declines exceeding 2% YoY for 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor demand, boosting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative when PAYEMS exceeds 150k for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4% signals tight labor markets, prompting wage inflation and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive when UNRATE stays below 4% for 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative when HOUST exceeds 1.4M for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong consumer demand, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative when both RSAFS and HOUST exceed thresholds for 2 consecutive weeks.
